title: Can an automation create a milestone?
excerpt:
Hello All, 
I have been exploring updating the timeline with various set dates using the Column Magic suite of automations. It has been working well enough so far, but because I have been setting the Start Date and the E…

posts.author: Stowl (Nicholas Lee)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello All,
I have been exploring updating the timeline with various set dates using the Column Magic suite of automations. It has been working well enough so far, but because I have been setting the Start Date and the End Date through the automation, Monday understandably interprets that an item takes 1 day if the Start Date and End Date are the same. Is there a way to make it the timeline interpret a Milestone when the Start and End Date are the same?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @Stowl

The API supports setting the timeline as a milestone. When writing the timeline through the API the developer can set the visualization_type to the string “milestone”, and have the start and end date the same. The timeline in your monday board will now show as a milestone.

If this is supported by the Column Magic app should be asked to the developers.

@mondayMan1 , this one is for you I believe

posts.author: Sjurd (Sjurd Skutlaberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Stowl
If you connect the timeline column to a duration (numbers column), and then enter 0 (zero) as the duration, it will change the timeline to a milestone. If timeline column is not populated, then it automatically set the milestone for today. If populated it choses the start date.
I’m not sure this is helpful for your case, but still interesting to know.

posts.author: WirkKarl (Karl)
posts.cookedHtml:
Nicholas Lee:

Hello All,
I have been exploring updating the timeline with various set dates using the Column Magic suite of automations. It has been working well enough so far, but because I have been setting the Start Date and the End Date through the automation, Monday understandably interprets that an item takes 1 day if the Start Date and End Date are the same. Is there a way to make it the timeline interpret a Milestone when the Start and End Date are the same?

Hello everyone,

I’ve been using Column Magic automations to set Start and End Dates on a timeline, and it’s mostly working well.

However, when the Start Date and End Date are the same, Monday shows it as a 1-day task — which makes sense. But in my case, I want that to be treated as a milestone, not a task.

Is there any way to make Monday treat same-day Start/End entries as milestones automatically through an automation?

Would love to hear if anyone has done this or found a workaround.

Thanks!

posts.author: Stowl (Nicholas Lee)
posts.cookedHtml: So simple but it works. I cannot believe I overlooked this. I guess I need to make some more exception automations.

posts.author: Stowl (Nicholas Lee)
posts.cookedHtml:
I created the automations setting duration to 0 when the start and end date match. Now I guess I have a visual bug. I can longer select milestone when I click on the timeline. The only way I can make I milestone now is by changing duration to 0.
I have tried different browsers, deleting my browsers cache, and restarting my computer. The affected template with the automations that creates milestones under the aforementioned circumstances is the only project that no longer has the milestone checkbox item.
It seems there is always something wrong.

posts.author: Sjurd (Sjurd Skutlaberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Stowl Did you try to turn the milestone back on in the column settings?
See screenshot.

Screenshot1190×406 35.1 KB

posts.author: Stowl (Nicholas Lee)
posts.cookedHtml: That did the trick thanks. I have no idea why working on a project would somehow change the what the template does, but it is now fixed!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @basdebruin, I’m not sure who mondayMan1 is but @jcorrell is my user.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Stowl, it seems that you found a solution. But I wanted to let you know that I am working to add a function to Column Magic that will do what you’re asking for.

Jim - The Monday Man
Column Magic

posts.author: gwsavuk (Graham Wright)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Jim. I may have misunderstood the use cases above, so to confirm - it it possible to copy changes to date columns (made manually by a user) into timelines with Column Magic? It appears not from the automation templates and my testing, but wanted to be sure. I know other apps can do this specifically, but IT dept compliance means I can currently only work within existing installed apps, such as the ever reliable Column Magic that’s been serving us beautifully for us for 2 years now. (Yes, I’m flattering you in the hope you can find a solution! )

posts.author: gwsavuk (Graham Wright)
posts.cookedHtml: @JCorrell Sorry didn’t tag you.

posts.author: Stowl (Nicholas Lee)
posts.cookedHtml:
@gwsavuk Wow I can answer this. Column Magic does has an automation option available to do this:

image601×242 8.87 KB

posts.author: gwsavuk (Graham Wright)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you Nicholas! I was looking in the templates rather than directly in a new automation for options there.

This has got me halfway there, but unfortunately I left out some key info, which is that I’m doing this at both Item and Subitem levels, and the automation doesn’t work with subitems (where in fact 90% of the project planning changes are being made on a daily basis). I guess I’ll have to keep trying to get a new app approved.

posts.author: Sjurd (Sjurd Skutlaberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
I found one (the only one) native pre made automation template that can change a timeline column based on a date column.
Search for “this other date”, and use this template:

Screenshot1344×2106 201 KB

Not sure that this will help, but interesting to know that it exists. It will not work on subitems though.
